            Own from Salesforce

            You are here:

            1. Salesforce Help
            2. Docs
            3. Own from Salesforce

            Own from Salesforce

            Own from Salesforce offers a suite of products that protect against data loss and corruption while helping you build scalable, efficient, and secure solutions. Manage security controls from a single view and monitor your data security posture. You can also address privacy requirements in production and development environments without slowing down the pace of work. Own from Salesforce also offers targeted data backup and recovery solutions for Salesforce, Microsoft, Workday, and ServiceNow.

            • Own from Salesforce Administration
              Own from Salesforce provides identity and access management, security, and other settings for Anonymize, Archive, Backup and Recover, Discover, and Seed. Manage global settings such as single sign on (SSO), multi-factor authentication (MFA), and role based access controls (RBAC). You can also access settings that help you respond to data privacy requests, manage your users, and more.
            • Anonymize
              Safeguard sensitive information by creating custom templates that mask sensitive data fields, such as phone numbers and addresses. With Anonymize, you can mask data when sharing sandbox data with other product teams for development, testing, or training. You can also populate empty fields with unique synthetic data. Anonymizing data reduces risk and helps your organization adhere to compliance and privacy regulations.
            • Archive Managed Package
              Archive by Own from Salesforce helps you manage growing data volumes and improve system performance by automatically archiving old or inactive data. You keep only important and reliable information in production. The Archive managed package not only enhances system performance by reducing data volume but also helps you maintain accurate and consistent data.
            • Backup & Recover for Salesforce
              Backup & Recover is designed to help you efficiently back up data, restore data if it's lost or corrupted, and meet data integrity and compliance goals. Precise data restoration tools help you identify and recover data affected by integration issues, system errors, or user error. You can create daily backups of data, attached files, metadata, managed packages, and sandboxes. Through secure cloud-to-cloud backups and independent data storage, stay connected to your backup data from anywhere without interruption.
            • Security Center Extension
              Security Center Extension by Own from Salesforce helps enhance data security and compliance. After installing the Security Center Extension managed package, use it to find sensitive data and manage data classification and user access settings. You can then decide how best to secure that data by using other Salesforce settings and features. The Who Sees What Explorer tab shows object and field-level access and permission assignments. On the Data Classification tab, find and classify sensitive fields such as personal information or financial data. The Security Insights tab helps you analyze your security posture and generates a risk-prioritized list of security gaps.
            • Shield Extension
              Shield Extension managed package by Own from Salesforce includes tools that help you get the most out of Shield Platform Encryption, Field Audit Trail, and Field History Tracking. The Platform Encryption Analyzer streamlines field-level encryption planning and implementation. Use the Field History Explorer to view history changes for tracked fields. With History Retention Policy Manager, monitor and update field history tracking and data retention configurations.
            • Secure
              Secure by Own from Salesforce is a managed package that helps you get more out of Salesforce security and access features. Gain insights into your data’s security posture and classify data for informed security policy management. Understand which users can access which data and navigate data retention and field history settings. Secure includes features now found in Security Center Extension and Shield Extension managed packages.
            • Seeding Data in Salesforce
              Streamline development and testing by seeding data from one org to another with the Seeding app. You can customize data copying into sandbox and production orgs. Create and configure seeding templates that define which records to add, update, or delete. Use filters to control the amount and type of data seeded so that your team can reproduce customer use cases and production scenarios.
            • Backup & Recover for Workday
              Backup & Recover for Workday efficiently backs up and restores data within the Workday environment. Help your organization meet audit and regulatory obligations when you back up data, files, and attachments. Integrated tools enable quick restoration of lost or corrupted data, maintaining data integrity and minimizing loss. You can configure data retention policies to comply with specific country or regional requirements.
            • Backup & Recover for Dynamics
              Backup & Recover for Dynamics provides enterprise-grade protection for Dynamics 365 and Power Platform data. Automatic daily backups create precise copies of all data and proactive monitoring alerts you of any suspect changes. Incidents are streamlined for quick recovery, so you can isolate data loss, understand its scope, and restore affected data. Configure backups for any number of production instances or sandboxes, regardless of their size or complexity. Maintain compliance with internal data retention policies, industry regulations, and government mandates.
            • Backup & Recover for ServiceNow
              Backup & Recover for ServiceNow provides granular recovery, on-demand backups, and longer retention periods to protect data and meet data backup and recovery requirements. Synthetic backups minimize resource consumption by reducing the need for frequent full backups. Recovery tools isolate and restore only what you need. You control the timing and availability of backups while proactive monitoring alerts you to data loss or corruption.
            • Seeding Data in ServiceNow
              Seeding for ServiceNow allows you to populate data sets from a production, sub-prod, or personal developer instance. Protect personally identifiable and sensitive information when you seed anonymized data using custom templates. Build templates that define a subset of related data to extract from a source instance, and use those templates to quickly and repeatedly seed any existing sub-prod with an identical data structure. Save time by automating recurring and one-time seeding jobs. Make sure that specific destinations are fully seeded with required data, in advance of upcoming sprints and development projects.
            Did this article solve your issue?
            Let us know so we can improve!

            Salesforce Help | Article
            We use cookies to make your interactions with our website more meaningful. They help us better understand how our websites are used, so we can tailor content for you. For more information about the different cookies we are using, read the Privacy Statement. To change your cookie settings and preferences, see the Cookie Consent Manager.

            Cookie Consent Manager

            Cookie Consent Manager

            • General Information

            • Required Cookies

            • Functional Cookies

            • Advertising Cookies

            General Information

            We use three kinds of cookies on our websites: required, functional, and advertising. You can choose whether functional and advertising cookies apply. Click on the different cookie categories to find out more about each category and to change the default settings.
            Privacy Statement

            Required Cookies

            Always Active

            Required cookies are necessary for basic website functionality. Some examples include: session cookies needed to transmit the website, authentication cookies, and security cookies.

            Functional Cookies

            Functional cookies enhance functions, performance, and services on the website. Some examples include: cookies used to analyze site traffic, cookies used for market research, and cookies used to display advertising that is not directed to a particular individual.

            Advertising Cookies

            Advertising cookies track activity across websites in order to understand a viewer’s interests, and direct them specific marketing. Some examples include: cookies used for remarketing, or interest-based advertising.
